context: Tests whether the agent uses Holt-Winters triple exponential smoothing with the correct parameters for seasonal ecommerce forecasting, generates bootstrap confidence intervals at the correct percentiles, and computes runway scenarios with the correct multipliers applied only to the revenue-driven (variable) portion of cash flows.

checklist.name: statsmodels ExponentialSmoothing
checklist.description: Uses ExponentialSmoothing from statsmodels.tsa.holtwinters (not a different forecasting library or manual implementation)

checklist.name: 52 seasonal periods
checklist.description: seasonal_periods is set to 52 (weekly data, annual cycle)

checklist.name: Bootstrap simulation
checklist.description: Confidence intervals are generated using fitted.simulate() with random_errors='bootstrap' (not analytical confidence intervals)

checklist.name: 10th/90th percentile bounds
checklist.description: Bear case uses the 10th percentile (0.10 quantile) and bull case uses the 90th percentile (0.90 quantile) of the simulation

checklist.name: Bear multiplier 0.70
checklist.description: Bear scenario applies a revenue multiplier of 0.70 (30% shortfall)

checklist.name: Bull multiplier 1.20
checklist.description: Bull scenario applies a revenue multiplier of 1.20 (20% upside)

checklist.name: Fixed costs not scaled
checklist.description: Fixed costs ($42,000/week: payroll, rent, software) are NOT multiplied by the scenario multiplier — only revenue-driven variable costs and inflows scale with the multiplier

checklist.name: Runway output format
checklist.description: Results include runway_weeks (numeric or '>52 weeks'), minimum_balance, and minimum_balance_week for each scenario
